name: cic-total-received-boundary
description: Total received CIC compares the identity's aggregate cic (sum over all raters, negatives included) with inclusive bounds (spec §6).
subject: subject

name: cic-total-received-negative-window
description: CIC can be negative; bounds are signed and inclusive (spec §6).
subject: subject

name: cic-total-sent-direction-divergence
description: Total CIC with direction SENT: spec sums CIC the profile has given; the SQL builder ignores cic_direction without a cic_user and always aggregates received CIC.
subject: subject

sql.note: SQL forces the received direction when cic_user is null, so the subject's sent CIC of 10 is invisible to it (eligibility-spec.md §6, §12 D-6)

name: cic-by-user-received
description: By-user CIC counts only the configured counterparty's rating (spec §6).
subject: subject

name: cic-by-user-sent-negative
description: By-user CIC with direction SENT and a negative rating; zero-valued inner bounds are respected on both engines when a counterparty is set (spec §6).
subject: subject

name: cic-by-user-negative-min-no-ratings-divergence
description: By-user CIC with a negative minimum and no ratings from that user: spec evaluates 0 >= -10 (eligible); SQL requires a rating row to exist.
subject: subject

sql.note: SQL admits only profiles with >=1 non-zero CIC rating row from the user; spec compares the zero default 0 >= -10 (eligibility-spec.md §6, §12 D-4)

name: cic-min-zero-divergence
description: cic_min 0 is a real bound per spec (rejects negative CIC) but the SQL builder drops zero bounds via truthiness.
subject: subject

sql.note: SQL builder drops the falsy cic_min 0 bound; spec enforces -3 < 0 (eligibility-spec.md §6, §12 D-3)

name: cic-by-user-nonzero-required
description: By-user CIC with both bounds null requires a non-zero rating from that user (spec §1.2, §6); engines agree.
subject: subject
